r_relatable_problem.primary_problem: Stock prices move based on company fundamentals, market sentiment, and investor decisions in a relatively free market. The Fed only influences broad conditions through interest rates.

s_specific_solution.core_mechanism: Fed Chairman Transition Stock Manipulation / Fed Bull Market Cycle

headline.text: FEDERAL RESERVE CONFESSES To Manipulating Stock Prices 7 Times in Last 72 Years... And they're doing it again in August 2006 with these 4 stocks...

schema_analysis.expectation_violated: Most investors believe the Fed's role is limited to interest rates and monetary policy. The idea that they actively manipulate specific stocks - and that this is documented and predictable - violates the fundamental assumption of 'free markets.'
schema_analysis.whitespace_territory: Positions Fed interventions as predictable, cyclical events tied to new chairmen - creating an 'insider knowledge' frame. Most financial advice focuses on fundamentals or technicals, not institutional manipulation patterns.

lead.full_text:
On August 8th, 2006, the Federal Reserve's plans will hijack the stock market... And boost the share price of 30 small companies. This historic event will grab the front-page headlines of every major financial publication in the country. In fact, right now the Fed's upcoming intervention is one of the most widely talked about and anticipated events among the world's top money managers. The timing couldn't be more perfect. The move is expected to jumpstart a struggling stock market...

Richard Bernstein, Chief Investment Strategist for Merrill Lynch, says, 'The Fed is a critical variable for 2006 [stocks].' USA Today reports, 'The fate of the market next year is likely to come from the Federal Reserve [intervention].'

If you're an investor, this is great news because you can make a lot of money by investing in just one of the 30 stocks pumped up by the Federal Reserve. In this special report, I'll tell you the top 4.

schema_analysis.expectation_violated: Most investment advice frames opportunities as analysis-based discoveries. This frames it as a scheduled, predictable event with specific dates - like having advance knowledge of an earthquake.
schema_analysis.whitespace_territory: Event-based investing tied to institutional patterns rather than company fundamentals or market trends. Creates 'insider calendar' positioning.

three_part_structure.what_they_think: Stock prices move based on company fundamentals, market sentiment, and investor decisions in a relatively free market. The Fed only influences broad conditions through interest rates.
three_part_structure.what_it_really_is: The Federal Reserve - a private corporation with minimal oversight - actively manipulates specific growth stocks every time they appoint a new chairman. This has happened 7 times since 1934, and it's predictable as clockwork.
three_part_structure.why_nothing_worked: You've been analyzing company fundamentals and following conventional investment wisdom while the real driver of market movements - Fed intervention tied to chairman appointments - has been operating invisibly. You were playing chess while they were moving the board.

narrative_reorganization.self_blame_removal: It's not that you lacked stock-picking skill or made poor timing decisions. The market has been moved by an institutional force that operates outside the analysis framework you were taught. Your stock picks may have been sound, but you didn't know about the hidden hand tilting the entire game board.
narrative_reorganization.coherence_creation: All those times the market moved against logic... when great companies underperformed... when you felt like insiders knew something you didn't... they ALL connect to this pattern of Fed manipulation tied to chairman transitions. The market isn't as 'free' as you thought.
narrative_reorganization.new_self_concept: Not a naive retail investor who doesn't 'get it,' but someone who now understands the institutional patterns that move markets - someone who can position themselves alongside the wealthy and connected who already know these cycles.

mechanism.name: Fed Chairman Transition Stock Manipulation / Fed Bull Market Cycle
proof_elements: Historical charts showing 7 Fed interventions since 1934 (charts for 1934, 1951, 1970, 1980, 1988, etc.)
1982 court case Lewis vs. United States establishing Fed as 'independent, privately owned and locally controlled corporations'
Dr. Murray Rothbard quote on Fed lack of accountability
Louis McFadden (Chairman of Banking and Currency Committee) congressional testimony calling Fed 'stock market manipulators'
Specific stock examples from past cycles (Computervision 1980: $39 to $85 in 9 months; C-COR Electronics 1988: 291% gain)
Expert quotes (Richard Bernstein/Merrill Lynch, USA Today, investment banker Kevin Johnston)
Ben Bernanke quote: 'I am confident the Fed would take whatever means necessary to prevent significant deflation'
Track record: 19 'no risk' recommendations since 2002, average gain 51.25%, 83.3% win rate

offer.price_anchoring: Regular price $199, now $99. Special reports valued at $199 each (4 reports = $796 value). Elite conference call service mentioned at $5,000. Total package valued at $1,000+
offer.guarantee: 100% satisfaction guarantee for 6 months - full refund, keep all reports. PLUS guarantee that top 5 'Fed-backed' stocks will double in 2006 or full refund.
bonuses: FREE: The Homerun Stocks of 2006: Three Stocks That Will Double This Year ($199 value)
FREE: How to Buy a Stock That's Safer Than a Bond ($299 value)
FREE: Investors' Paychecks: The Easy, Safe Way to High Yields ($199 value)
FREE: The Lost Secret of No Risk Investing ($199 value)
FREE: Daily email updates (The Blast + Daily Wealth)
FREE: 6 years of research archive access

fssit_candidates.statement: In fact, most Americans have no idea that the Federal Reserve is actually a private corporation... In a landmark court case, Lewis vs. United States (1982), the United States Court of Appeals for the 9th circuit ruled that the Federal Reserve banks are 'independent, privately owned and locally controlled corporations.'
fssit_candidates.past_explaining_power: Validates the nagging suspicion that something fundamental about 'how the system works' has been hidden from them. The Fed isn't what they thought it was - it's a private entity, not a government agency. This explains why they've felt like outsiders.

fssit_candidates.statement: The fact is, if you're working with an investment management company, it's always going to be in their best interests to only give you their worst ideas. The billions they manage are going into their second best ideas. And their own money is going into their top ideas.
fssit_candidates.past_explaining_power: Explains why following professional advice hasn't worked as promised - the system is designed to give retail investors the leftovers. They weren't failing at investing; they were getting the scraps while insiders feasted.

fssit_candidates.statement: The main idea behind 'no risk' investing is to find companies whose shares are so safe and whose business is so solid that it would take a national disaster for you to lose money. Once you learn how Wall Street really works, you won't be surprised to learn that Wall Street almost never recommends 'no risk' stocks to its clients. Why? Because investors don't need to trade in and out of these shares (there's no commission revenue.)
fssit_candidates.past_explaining_power: This is THE KEY FSSIT. Explains why they've been steered toward volatile stocks and frequent trading - it wasn't about their returns, it was about Wall Street's commissions. The safest opportunities are hidden because they don't generate fees. Every trade recommendation they got now makes sense through this lens.

fssit_candidates.statement: The Federal Reserve always intervenes in the market right after they appoint a new chairman... Every time the Fed elects a new chairman, stock prices explode - that's every Fed chairman since 1934.
fssit_candidates.past_explaining_power: Reorganizes their entire understanding of market cycles. Those 'bull markets' they thought were organic economic growth? Fed manipulation. Those times they missed the boat on big gains? They didn't know the calendar. This is a complete reframe of causation.

rsf_analysis.fssit_placement_pattern: Front-loaded with Fed revelation in lead, then reinforced throughout with Wall Street conflicts of interest and pattern explanations. Multiple reframes stack: Fed is private → Wall Street steers you wrong → cycles are predictable → you've been on the outside.
